messages.content: hi
Hi! How can I help you today? I'm ready to assist with any software engineering tasks in the K_MIND project.
can you read my claude desktop session from this app ?

The K_MIND repository is currently empty — it only has a .gitattributes file with no application code yet. No, this app cannot read your Claude Desktop session. There's no code in this repository at all right now — it's a fresh repo with just an initial commit.
can you keep each of my comments and yours on filesystem in realtime during this session ?
Asked user about preferred file format (JSON vs Markdown) and storage location. User chose JSON for performance and sessions/ folder.
which type of file is the faster for you to read and to write : json or markdown ?

JSON is faster for both reading and writing — it's structured data with no formatting ambiguity, so there's less processing overhead. Appending to a Markdown file requires more care with formatting and line breaks. That said, the difference is negligible for a conversation log. But if speed is the priority, I'll use JSON.
yes, json will be. can you have two major data group in this json file: near_memory and far_memory ?
Asked user what near_memory and far_memory should contain. User chose to explain their vision.

actually, for performance, it may have to be two seperate files. looking to persist all in far_memory: integral comments inputs and output from user and from claude. near_memory, i see it as summaries maintain also in real time from conversation of user and of claude. near_memory have references/pointers to far_memory and to another external file which would be called mind_memory. mind_memory is a mermaid mindmap file maintain in real time with the other two file as the core mind. let's build a quick prototype. since mind_memory is the subconscious mind role, this is the key file for this prototype. let's create that core file after you have completed the skeleton code. mind_memory is in a folder at same level of sessions folder named mind. mind_memory have 1 central main element for now which is knowledge.

Presented the 3-file architecture plan: mind/mind_memory.md (mermaid mindmap, core), sessions/far_memory.json (full conversation), sessions/near_memory.json (summaries with pointers). User approved the plan.
so, all these files are going to be updated in real time conversation and results from work in a session ?

Yes, exactly. Every turn from now on, I'll update all 3 files: far_memory.json (append messages verbatim), near_memory.json (add summary with pointers), mind_memory.md (grow mindmap with new knowledge nodes). This includes conversation and any work results — code written, files created, errors encountered, decisions made.
what does constraints means in the mind ?

The constraints node captures limitations and boundaries — things the system cannot do. Currently has one child: no_cross_session_access (Claude Code can't read Claude Desktop sessions). Think of it as the mind knowing its own limits. New constraints discovered during work would be added there.

can you make claude start this architecture on beginning, on resuming and on resume from compaction the user and claude session ? can we define architectural components from other components that will be more from the work session ? I would add session directly connected to knowledge and i forecast child nodes of session node been more dynamic. so right now, i see a more static structure with the architecture node tree that will change with the architecture system. constraints is dynamic but base on conception decisions. and, session which is going to be dynamic. i have more node groups to explain for work node directly connected to knowledge which is the result of the session work been staged to approval and for conventions to reuse as the user and claude build or work on something. so, with this mind map, this is exactly the purpose, see the hive at one sight and refer to files for knowledge persisted. we should expect session and work child nodes to have pointers/references to persisted knowledge in near and in far memory files.

Identified 6 top-level groups under knowledge: architecture (static), constraints (semi-dynamic), session (dynamic), work (dynamic), conventions (growing), documentation (TBD). Will auto-start via CLAUDE.md. Inline refs [far:N,near:N] for session and work nodes. Asked user about file naming — chose domain-specific names.

so claude should read mind as the primary reference and then dig to persisted knowledge on files to keep minimal usage of claude memory. work is to remember on what we are working on and refer to it accurately in work force session tree brain storming by the user and by claude. we should add then the other top level group which is documentation which will hold the mind map of the documentation structure that is going to be defined later in this prototype conception.

Confirmed mind-first strategy: Claude reads mind_memory.md as primary reference, digs into files only when needed. Work node tracks active work and brainstorming. Added documentation as 6th top-level group. Updated plan.

each top level group have a folder at same level of session with at least one json file for persisted references. these as well are holders for data session and references to other files persisted on the system sometimes.

Confirmed: each top-level mindmap group maps to its own folder with a domain-specific JSON file. Directory structure: architecture/architecture.json, constraints/constraints.json, work/work.json, conventions/conventions.json, documentation/documentation.json. User chose domain-specific naming.
session files are more the dynamic brainstorming that are using accomplished work as reference for concordance and for continuity of working and of building activities.

Confirmed: session files (far_memory, near_memory) are the dynamic brainstorming record. They reference accomplished work in work/ for concordance and continuity of building activities. Work is the stable anchor point. Plan approved and implementation started.

can you build a skill that will be called by user and by claude to output the reduced mind_memory map without architecture and constraints trees ? output after the mindmap the results of the context call. this skill is to be called at start, resume or return from compaction of the user session.
Researched Claude Code custom skills system. Designed /mind-context skill at .claude/skills/mind-context/SKILL.md. User requested full mode option added.
add also a full option for full mind map presentation

Added full mode: /mind-context shows reduced mindmap (dynamic nodes only), /mind-context full shows complete mindmap with all 6 groups. Built the skill, updated CLAUDE.md lifecycle events to use /mind-context.

first convention to define for the mindmap: on full display and markdown github file: half/half top down disposition — displayed half down diagram: architectural, constraints, conventions, all top level groups that are system related — displayed half top diagram: session, work, documentation. on normal mindmap output: half/half left right disposition — on the left: the session group — on the right: the work and the documentation groups.

Defined first convention: mindmap display layout. Full display uses top-down half/half (session,work,doc top; architecture,constraints,conventions bottom). Normal display uses left-right half/half (session left; work,documentation right). Reordered mindmap source nodes accordingly.

add on the mindmap convention, theme colors, first one to define is light: top level group: more solid blue colors, fonts are white bold uppercase. child tree nodes of top level group: tend to lighter blue at each depth level, boxes are light blue from depth level, fonts are dark blue bold uppercase. inline references lowercase, not bold, with respective box color font.

Defined light theme convention: top-level nodes solid blue with white bold uppercase fonts. Child nodes progressively lighter blue per depth with dark blue bold uppercase fonts. Inline refs lowercase, not bold, font color matches box color. Added to conventions.json and mindmap. Updated SKILL.md with display layout instructions for both modes.
see picture, this is what i get in github markdown file. [showed rendered mermaid mindmap screenshot from GitHub — dark theme, left-right radial layout]

Observed mermaid mindmaps are always left-right radial, no top-down option. Updated layout convention to left-right for both modes. Added mermaid %%{init}%% theme directive with light blue color scheme: primaryColor #1565C0 (solid blue), secondaryColor #42A5F5 (medium blue), tertiaryColor #90CAF9 (light blue), with white text on primary and dark blue text on secondary/tertiary.
call mind-context for me now

Skill tool didn't find /mind-context (not registered in this session). Executed manually: read mind_memory.md and near_memory.json, presented normal mode output showing left-side dynamic nodes (session, work, documentation) plus last 3 near_memory summaries (8, 9, 10). Confirmed active K_MIND session.
